tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction. The presence of glucose and protein in urine despite normal blood glucose indicates renal rather than diabetic etiology. Cloudy, brown urine with ammoniacal odor may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
